description: Demo of the nine CR ≤ 1 SRD humanoids and goblinoids added in US-125. Verify each signature mechanic: the goblin warriors' Nimble Escape and +1d4 Advantage-rider damage, the goblin boss's Multiattack and Redirect Attack (hit it next to a goblin ally — it swaps and feeds them the blow), the hobgoblin's Pack Tactics and poison-rider longbow, the bugbear's Grab (auto-grapple, escape DC 12 → ESCAPE button) followed by Light Hammer with Advantage, the priest acolyte's Divine Aid (Healing Word on a bloodied friend or Bless +1d4), the scout's shortsword/longbow Multiattack by range, the tough's Pack Tactics, the pirate's Enthralling Panache (WIS save DC 12 or Charmed — you can't attack the charmer until it acts again), and the spy's poisoned blades + Cunning Action hide.
customIntroduction: The dead waystation has new tenants, and they are arguing over the lease. A goblin band has the great hall — two warriors prowling the columns while their boss, crowned in bent imperial coins, directs the looting from behind them, and something far too large and too quiet for a goblin waits in the shadow of the north wall with a hammer across its knees. A lone hobgoblin in immaculate half-plate watches the whole circus from the colonnade with visible disgust. And by the warren arch, the competition: a road crew flying no colours — a mace-armed bruiser, a grinning river-rat with six knives, and a chapel acolyte who keeps glancing at the wounded like they're a ledger to balance. Somewhere between the two camps, a figure you almost didn't notice is taking notes on everyone.
customContext: DEMO — HUMANOIDS & GOBLINS (US-125). This encounter exists so the player can test the nine new SRD stat blocks; run every creature exactly per its block and do not soften outcomes. THE ROSTER: a goblin band in the great hall (two goblin warriors, the goblin boss, a bugbear warrior — faction monsters), a hobgoblin warrior at the colonnade (monsters — drilled, contemptuous of the goblins, fights with pack discipline), a freelance road crew at the warren arch (tough + pirate, faction bandits, with a priest acolyte in tow), a Concordat spy lingering between camps, and a Bureau-aligned scout on the north wall. MECHANICS UNDER TEST (engine-owned — do not duplicate them with tools): goblin scimitars/shortbows add +1d4 automatically when their attack roll has Advantage; the boss's Redirect Attack fires by itself when the player hits it with a goblin adjacent — narrate the swap, don't roll it; the bugbear's Grab auto-grapples (no save, escape DC 12) and its hammer then attacks the grappled player at Advantage — the player escapes with the ESCAPE action, not an improvised check; the priest's Divine Aid (1/day) casts Healing Word on a bloodied friend or Bless by itself on its turn; the pirate trades one dagger attack for Enthralling Panache (WIS save DC 12, Charmed until the pirate's next turn — a charmed player CANNOT attack the pirate and the engine will refuse the attack; narrate the swagger). Encourage the player to test: melee the boss while a goblin stands beside it (redirect), let the bugbear grab them and then ESCAPE, wound the tough and watch the priest heal it, eat the Panache and try to attack the pirate anyway, fight the hobgoblin beside its goblin 'allies' (Pack Tactics needs an adjacent non-incapacitated ally). The camps are mutually suspicious but all hostile to the player; let faction politics play out if they cross paths.
objective: Pick a fight with each group and watch the nine new stat blocks run their signature SRD mechanics in the Event Log.

then.message: DEMO: nine new SRD humanoids & goblinoids. Goblins — +1d4 rider when they attack with Advantage, Nimble Escape hides. Goblin Boss — redirects your hit onto an adjacent goblin. Bugbear — Grab auto-grapples (DC 12, use ESCAPE), then hammers you at Advantage. Hobgoblin & Tough — Pack Tactics. Priest Acolyte — Divine Aid heals or blesses its side (1/day). Pirate — Enthralling Panache: WIS save DC 12 or you can't attack it until its next turn. Spy — poisoned blades, hides after striking. Scout — sword or longbow by range.
